key_themes: Q1 earnings beat overshadowed by soft Q2 guidance
Reed Hastings stepping down from the board
Strong advertising momentum on track to double to $3B in 2026
Buy-the-dip views vs. valuation and growth concerns
summary: Netflix delivered a solid Q1 2026 beat with revenue of $12.25B (up 16% YoY) and EPS of $1.23 (helped by a $2.8B one-time termination fee), while maintaining full-year guidance for 12-14% revenue growth and strong ad progress. However, Q2 guidance came in slightly below expectations and Reed Hastings' planned board exit triggered sharp investor disappointment, sending shares down nearly 10%. Sentiment is currently negative in the near term due to the guidance miss and sell-off, though many analysts view current levels near $97 as a buying opportunity given Netflix's competitive moat, engagement initiatives (AI, vertical video, games), and long-term growth runway.[[1]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/netflix-nflx-earnings-q1-2026.html)[[2]](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/netflix-stock-nflx-q1-2026-earnings/)[[3]](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-16/netflix-revenue-beats-estimates-as-hastings-steps-down)
sources: 25+ news articles on Q1 2026 earnings, 20+ recent X posts referencing $NFLX or Netflix stock, Analyst notes from Needham, Deutsche Bank, Wolfe Research, others
